que: What does the Placeholder widget draw by default?
options: A box with a dashed border and an X

que: What is the main purpose of the Placeholder widget?
options: Mark where a widget will be added later

que: When is Placeholder primarily intended to be used?
options: During development to visualize layout structure

que: What is the default color of the Placeholder border and X?
Which constructor parameter controls the color of the border and X?
What is the default value of strokeWidth in the constructor?
What does the strokeWidth property control?
options: The width of the border lines
The font size of the X label

que: What is the default fallbackWidth used when the widget is unbounded?
What is the default fallbackHeight used when the widget is unbounded?
In the example, what color is used for the Placeholder?
In the example, what is the value of strokeWidth?
In the example, what are the values of fallbackHeight and fallbackWidth?
Which statement about fallbackWidth and fallbackHeight is correct?
options: They are ignored if constraints are unbounded
They are used when the widget is unbounded
They only apply when color is null

que: What is the recommended usage guidance for Placeholder in production?
options: Should not be used in production UIs

que: Which of these is a good use case for Placeholder?
options: Final button design in a released app
Temporary marker for a widget to be implemented later

que: What type of widget is Placeholder conceptually?
options: A layout widget that arranges children
A visual debugging aid for layouts

que: Which parameter would you modify to make the Placeholder occupy more space when unconstrained?
What visual shape does Placeholder most closely resemble?
options: A circle with a plus sign
A box with a dashed border and an X

que: Why is Placeholder useful during development?
options: It helps visualize the layout structure before real widgets are added

que: Which statement best summarizes the Placeholder widget?
options: A layout manager for complex grids
A simple visual box with an X used during development to mark where widgets will go, with configurable color, strokeWidth, and fallback size
